relations.note: Coral restriction implements the tertiary-as-accent-only aspect of the palette hierarchy.
Chart role coloring applies the blues-dominant / gold-reserved ratio within charts.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Preserve the intended brand color balance.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate, while tertiary colors serve only as accents and surfaces.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Ensure consistent, legible text and link color across background contexts.
rules.rule_text: Headlines/headings use Brand Blue (headline.color = #00529b) on light backgrounds, or White on dark/gradient backgrounds. Body text uses Graphite (body.color = #212121) on light backgrounds, or White with increased line height on dark backgrounds. Links are Brand Blue (#00529b) underlined on light backgrounds, White underlined on dark. Light/dark variant switching is carried by the tokens.

quotes: **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**
Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Keep gold emphasis meaningful and standardize chart data coloring.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) are dominant; Sunshine/Gold (#faa31b / #ffc60a) is strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures; gray is used for placebo.

quotes: **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Standardize callout background surfaces by callout type.
rules.rule_text: Callout surfaces use Light Blue (#E6F0F9) at 70% tint for data callouts, and Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) at 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.

effect.reserved_for: sparing tertiary accent only — not primary CTAs, headlines, or data
effect.subject: accent.coral.usage

quotes: **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Constrain Coral to rare accent use.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only and must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. [GENERAL] Avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

quotes: **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances**
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Protect the locked logo gold transparency treatment.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / Gold (#ffc60a) must be maintained in all instances — in the logo and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic.
